Personal Learning Project

{{ home.title }}

A Rust reverse proxy project focused on module pipelines, ingress composition, and understanding how traffic moves at the edge.

Get Started Browse Docs
Built To Learn
Still Experimental
01 // Rationale

Why This Exists.

KSBH exists to learn by building: routing, modules, runtime behavior, and configuration models worked through directly in code.

01

Architectural Clarity

Make the moving parts of a reverse proxy visible instead of treating them as a black box.

02

Module Pipeline Study

Study global and ingress-local request processing as composable steps.

03

Rust Implementation

Build the runtime in Rust and understand the tradeoffs directly from the implementation.

04

Ingress Composition

Compare local file configuration with Kubernetes-native resource models.

02 // Data Pathway

Request Flow

01 inbox Request
02 route Route Match
03 public Global Modules
04 login Ingress Modules
05 dns Backend
03 // System Modules

Core Pieces

Runtime

The runtime handles routing, module execution, backend proxying, and runtime state updates.

Modules

  • http to https
  • robots.txt
  • oidc
  • proof of work
  • rate limit

Config Providers

File-based and Kubernetes-native configuration both feed the same routing and module model.

04 // Knowledge Base

Documentation

Getting Started + Module Specifications + Configuration + Developer Guide +

Start Here.

Read through the project, try it locally, and see how the pieces fit together.

GitHub Repo Codeberg
ksbh

Built to learn reverse proxy architecture, request modules, and runtime composition.
